rationale: Option (b) — classify future-day events as FUTURE_SCHEDULED at the parse stage, skip registry check entirely, eliminate verbose log noise. This is lower-risk than expanding the registry window (option a) because: (1) registry semantics remain unchanged (it still holds only today's games), (2) pre-game trading is not in scope for this system (the bot runs inference only on live games per generate_recommendation_for_game entry gates), (3) future events are correctly identified before expensive registry lookups and logging occur. The fix transforms ~40 verbose 'no_registry_match' logs per run (each with full registry dump) into a single summary line.
bot_pregame_trading_notes: Unable to verify bot behavior (bot_core.py / core/risk.py outside allowed_files). However, recommendation_api.py entry point generate_recommendation_for_game() only processes events where is_live=true (line 129-136), indicating live-only trading. Future-scheduled classification aligns with this design.
log_lines_before_sample: [2026-04-18 17:35:00] INFO NOT-LIVE [no_registry_match] event='mlb-bal-kc-2026-04-20' date=2026-04-20 parsed=(BAL vs KC) registry_games=[('CIN', 'MIN', 'live', True), ('KCR', 'NYY', 'live', True), ('NYM', 'CHC', 'live', True)]
[2026-04-18 17:35:01] INFO NOT-LIVE [future_dated_slug] event='mlb-bal-kc-2026-04-20' date=2026-04-20 parsed=(BAL vs KC) registry_match=BAL@KC status=pre game is_live=False
[repeated 39 more times with similar verbose output...]
log_lines_after_sample: [2026-04-18 17:35:00] DEBUG Skip future_scheduled: event='mlb-bal-kc-2026-04-20' date=2026-04-20 parsed=(BAL vs KC)
[2026-04-18 17:35:01] DEBUG Skip future_scheduled: event='mlb-stl-mia-2026-04-20' date=2026-04-20 parsed=(STL vs MIA)
[2026-04-18 17:35:05] INFO Discovery: 3 live | skipped: 12 non-mlb/date, 5 closed, 18 non-moneyline/no-keyword, 2 unparseable, 8 not-live, 40 future_scheduled, 1 duplicate_event_market

changes_summary.added_list: future_scheduled_slugs (collects event slugs for summary log)
changes_summary.new_check: After team parsing, before registry lookup: if game_date > today, classify as FUTURE_SCHEDULED, skip registry check, log at DEBUG level
changes_summary.removed: Redundant game_date > today check that was at line 552-563 (now unreachable)
changes_summary.updated_summary_log: Added future_scheduled count to Discovery summary line, added separate INFO line listing future event slugs if count > 0

no_changes_to: Registry data source or population logic
Bot entry gates or recommendation generation logic
next_steps: Deploy and observe shadow_engine.log. Expected improvement: 40 verbose 'no_registry_match' logs per run → 0 per run, replaced with 1 summary line. No change in discovery of live (same-day) games.
